UNITED STATES SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION

Washington, D.C. 20549

FORM 10-K

ANNUAL REPORT PURSUANT TO SECTION 13 OR 15(d) OF THE SECURITIES EXCHANGE ACT OF 1934

For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025

SYNTHETIC INDUSTRIES, INC.

(Exact name of registrant as specified in its charter)

TABLE OF CONTENTS

Item 1. Business ............ 3

Item 1A. Risk Factors ............ 8

Item 7. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations ............ 25

Item 8. Financial Statements and Supplementary Data ............ 40

PART I

Item 1. Business

Synthetic Industries, Inc. ("Synthetic," "we," "our," or the "Company") is a hypothetical entity used solely to exercise the section-extraction logic in this test suite. We design and manufacture nothing. Our reported revenue is zero. Our products do not exist. We exist only inside this HTML file.

The Company operates in one reportable segment: testing. Our principal markets are: developer machines, continuous-integration runners, and the README screenshots of portfolio projects. We have no employees and no competitors, although we are mildly threatened by changes to BeautifulSoup default parser behavior.

Our business strategy is to remain parseable. We achieve this by emitting clear, regex-friendly section headers and by avoiding the deeply nested table-of-contents structures that real 10-Ks abuse.

Seasonality: none. Backlog: none. Government contracts: none. Patents: none. Trademarks: SYNTHETIC(R) is a fictional mark.

Item 1A. Risk Factors

Investing in our common stock involves a high degree of fictional risk. You should carefully consider the following risk factors, which are also fictional, before making any investment decision in this hypothetical security.

We may fail to be parsed correctly. If the section extractor in this project misidentifies the start of Item 1A, the body of our Risk Factors will be attributed to the wrong section. This would cause our test suite to fail and prompt remediation work.

We depend heavily on a single regex. Our entire detection surface is anchored on one regular expression. Changes to that regex by a future maintainer could materially impair our ability to be located within an HTML document.

We may be confused with a table of contents entry. Many 10-Ks include both a TOC reference and a body header for each Item. Tools that naively take the first match will return TOC entries instead of body content. We mitigate this risk by being short, so that the body is the last match for each item header.

Macroeconomic conditions are irrelevant. Because we have no revenue, no costs, and no balance sheet, we are unusually well-insulated from interest-rate cycles, FX moves, and tariff regimes.

PART II

Item 7. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

The following discussion is fictional and should be read in conjunction with our (also fictional) consolidated financial statements. Forward-looking statements herein are guaranteed to be wrong because they describe a company that does not exist.

Results of Operations. Revenue for fiscal 2025 was zero, compared to zero in fiscal 2024, an increase of zero percent. Cost of revenue was also zero. Gross margin was undefined. Operating expenses consisted entirely of the disk space occupied by this HTML file.

Liquidity and Capital Resources. The Company holds no cash. We believe our existing resources, consisting of pytest fixtures, are sufficient to meet our anticipated needs for the foreseeable future, which we define as the lifespan of this repository.

Critical Accounting Estimates. We make no estimates. We have no revenue recognition policy. We do not capitalize software development costs because we have no software.

Item 8. Financial Statements and Supplementary Data

The consolidated financial statements of Synthetic Industries, Inc., together with the report of our independent registered public accounting firm (none retained), are not included in this filing because no such statements exist.

See pages F-1 through F-37 of this report for the financial statements, which intentionally do not exist.

End of synthetic 10-K.
